channel.title: Comments for page "ㄗitch 卄aven"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "ㄗitch 卄aven"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still go back and read these for fun sometimes, I do hope the author rises from their grave one day to bestow upon us lowly peasants another wonderous Pitch Haven entry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know this has already been answered somewhere on Tumblr, but I’m having trouble with finding it. That said, what are the names of all thirteen crafters, exactly?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thoroughly LOVED Pitch Black.
It was an awesome combination of horror, gore, mythological events (a personal favourite of mine in SCP matters), drama, tales, love story and wholesomness.
I've just finished binge-reading the whole affair in a one night stand, because it was so damn catching.
Beautiful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One of 682 test log entries has him referencing Pitch Haven's Eden as a place he was banished from.
So that's my headcanon so far.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, last comment was 5 months ago, I feel late to the party.

Anyways, I just recently found your work by randomly stumbling into a Reddit post with fanart of one of your SCPs. I have to say, I can't believe I read all, ALL OF IT, in just one day. I was just so hooked up, I couldn't stop. Haven't had this problem since I read the Anti-Memetics Division Hub. I hope that you can continue doing this amazing work. No Safe Haven was the best!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If Haven was an annoying place, we would all Ditch Haven.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If Pitch Haven was a virtual world inhabited by computer viruses, it would be called Glitch Haven.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If Pitch Haven featured persons who used magicks it would be Witch Haven.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If there were a swing song about Pitch Haven, it'd be called Puttin' on the Ritz Haven.

item.title: Regarding the recent page revision
item.content:encoded: Just an FYI, I went ahead and changed the audio player from Flash to HTML5, as Flash is rapidly losing support and already can't be viewed by many people, including Firefox and Mobile users. No content changes were made, and the article is otherwise unchanged.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, this post may sound random and completely out of the blue, but I don't know where else to express this. I LOVE Clovis. She is without a doubt my favorite character in the whole SCP universe! She's mysterious but also lovable. Believe me when I say that I REALLY want to see more of her!

EDIT: I have no idea why I thought this weird comment was a good idea. Please pay no attention to it. Instead, I'll simply say that I think Fantem's work is amazing, I hope more gets posted soon, ect., and leave it at that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We believe in you! (｀-´)>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If the author would get off her ass and write, Pitch Haven would probably be done by now. But the bitch gave-in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If Pitch Haven had little to no widespread appeal, it'd be Niche Haven.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If Pitch Haven were about Spider-Man, it'd be Pitch Kraven.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If Pitch Haven was a company that made roads with old trailer connectors, it'd be Hitch Paven'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If Pitch Haven were more ironic and irreverent, it'd be Kitsch Haven.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If the characters from Pitch Haven got sent into the ER, it would be Stitch Haven.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If all the characters were female canines, it'd be bitch haven

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: if pitch haven was livestreamed it would be twitch haven

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So. The one thing i have to ask is that has the serpent been confirmed as… something… yet? I feel as if he is just kinda missing, a plot hole, of sorts, and the only one i can think of in this canon. There is nothing truly about him, he has only been mention, and rarely at that. We have no idea what it is, only what it's "criminal act" was. And even then, we didn't get a "report" or "sorry letter" on its punishment. I'd like to know more about the thing that caused Eden to become a hell hole.

item.title: 2 new tales
item.content:encoded:
Sarah's Hard-Hitting Story, Tooth and Nail

Stuart's Book-Length story, No Safe Haven

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for the stories, especially No Safe Haven (even if it's not on the main site yet). They're wonderful. It's rare that something makes me feel simultaneously like I want more, but have closure of the story.

I'm an active reader of the SCP wiki in general, though I haven't been logged in on this site in years. I stayed up to 4am reading all the articles and stories here.

I logged back in this morning to upvote this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We believe in you Fantem!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Added a bit to the hub for things to come.

Also, changed the pics of Clovis, Stuart, Sarah, Aggie, and Freddie!

item.title: Re: A little confused...
item.content:encoded: Ah that clears it up a little bit thanks

item.title: Re: A little confused...
item.content:encoded:
"Honorable" is a caste of Eden, introduced and explained in SCP-2746.

"Greek letter class" simply refers to their personality type, as far as I can tell. It's unclear if they correspond to any real psychiatric personality assessment.

Both of these terms show up in the character bios on the hub page; these bios describe the main cast of Pitch Haven.

item.title: A little confused...
item.content:encoded:
I've followed this set of tales for a while but the main thing that puzzles me is how all the "Honorable"-s and "[INSERT GREEK LETTER]-class means in the hub, and online search has left me even more puzzled

Don't get me wrong, I LOVE this series, just a lil confused is all

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've been following this series for a while now, and I pretty much joined the site to comment on it. You've done a fantastic job, Fantem, and I eagerly await the next part.

I read the comments on several SCP articles in the series, which pointed out there was some hidden hexadecimal text, only viewable in the source. I had some free time this week and decided to translate the hidden text, from hexadecimal to ASCII First Language, and then into English using WrongJohnSilver's cipher here. I PM'ed Fantem to ask if it's okay to share them.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Any method of communication is acceptable.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Do you mean PMing her, or specifically calling for her on chat?

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: This is Fantem's article, and so you really should be asking her for anything regarding it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would anyone mind if I replace the blackbox covering Clovis' first SCP designation with SCP-2999-A? I ask this because the picture is the 2999 version rather than the 1913 one.

item.title: Re: This is probably rambling, but...
item.content:encoded: Then I wish you good luck in your endeavors and am eagerly looking forward to it, once you come out of your slump. And don't worry about the lack of motivation, it happens to everyone, and it always wears off. Thanks for the great stories and thanks to all those that helped you.

item.title: Re: This is probably rambling, but...
item.content:encoded: No, I can't have someone write it for me. It's something I gotta do myself, you know?

item.title: Re: This is probably rambling, but...
item.content:encoded: If you're low on energy, maybe you could send the ideas to someone and they can make an outline, and then you check it and edit it, then after polishing the details upload it. I'm sure there are many who would love to help you, and if you can't find any, I'm available as a backup… But first I'll have to figure out how to use the sandbox and PM system.

item.title: Re: This is probably rambling, but...
item.content:encoded: Yeah I've actually been working on that one currently. I have the idea stuff down, it's just… a lack of self-motivation I guess.

item.title: Re: This is probably rambling, but...
item.content:encoded: Maybe it's a lack of ideas? If you need one, Crowley's SCP designation still isn't revealed…

item.title: Re: This is probably rambling, but...
item.content:encoded: It'll come.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just sat down and read through this whole series chronologically. Really enjoy the interconnected stories and canon. Hope it's continued in the future.

item.title: This is probably rambling, but...
item.content:encoded:
God… Looking back at this, after so long, I don't know how to feel anymore.

Reading all the articles and tales all over again's giving me this weird pang of regret, especially after not contributing to Pitch Haven in fuck knows how long.

There hasn't been a single day where I don't think about this series. I genuinely miss writing for this, and I don't have the slightest idea what's keeping me from diving back in and making skips again. After so much drama in real life, a lot could be attributed to it. What did I have back then that I don't have right now? Whatever it was, I need it back. Bad.

I guess I don't really know. I want nothing more than to get back into it again. Maybe I'll swing by chat again in a few. Maybe getting back in touch with some good friends will get me motivated again and continue the series proper.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wish I understood enough about Pitch Haven to be comfortable writing for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, I myself have written SCP-2988 and SCP-2905, both of which are a separate storyline from the main Stuart/Sarah one. As long as you reveiw your draft with Fantem (and maybe other writers for the canon; I know I'd love to see potential stuff :3), I'm sure she'd be more than happy to see new characters added.

But ask Fantem personally for sure XD .-.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I add to the series do I have to focus on the main characters or can I write about secondary characters ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Scorpion451 said:

The vibe I get from the Maker is that he was a powerful but mortal reality warper who tried to create his vision of utopia in a pocket universe. It went badly- but in a very interesting and informative way.

Fantem has said that the Maker is literally God, in a few places, including this thread.

Also, if he was just a human "reality warper" then all of what happened in Eden was a farce, making the whole story less satisfying, IMO. The Crafters believe they invented life in all its specific myriad forms, for example, but your interpretation means they were just unwittingly copying evolution (because they were programmed to?). It just becomes a scripted performance of the Biblical myth, rather than the true root of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added SCP-2905 to the hub!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice, bu i must ask what SCP is the serpent that Mr Fred tried to kill?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added Fourteen Thirteen to the hub!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pitch Haven has become just about my favourite thing on the site. I really appreciate that it manages to draw on and directly reference modern religion without that connection ever becoming the blunt instrument that it does in so many other things. It never stops it from being itself or from being an SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"The human mind will always rely on one of three basic descriptions to describe that which they cannot fully explain themselves: magic, religion, or a science that is simply unknown to them. 'And' never seems to make an appearance…"

Demons, Legends, and Fables: Contextualized,
-Dr. Stuart Hayward, 19██

"If you put quotes around something and a name after it, it surprisingly doesn't automatically make it important."
— Doug Walker (As The Nostalgia Critic)

That's the only feedback I have for the hub, but as for the tales/SCPs, they are great reads.

EDIT: Don't take the feedback too seriously T^T

item.title: Re: Possible link mistake?
item.content:encoded:
Well, technically, 1913-2 is really only made of her, among other things.

(Also, I love that name. Good on ya. U>w<U)

item.title: Re: Possible link mistake?
item.content:encoded: Ah, ok. So Clovis is both the online rabbit and the 'dog-a-pult' (31-2). I get it now.

item.title: Re: Possible link mistake?
item.content:encoded: There's no mistake, at least not here. This is very intentional. Clovis went through a couple bodies before she went online.

item.title: Re: Possible link mistake?
item.content:encoded: Yes, that is also Clovis. What do you think is a mistake?

item.title: Re: Possible link mistake?
item.content:encoded: I'm refering to Clovis's 'normal' manifestation-which SunnyClockwork's art also says is Clovis (Go here: http://www.scp-wiki.net/sp-art-pitch-haven, scroll to 2999 and click Clovis 2-it's the same image).

item.title: Re: Possible link mistake?
item.content:encoded: Clovis is all of these things (and I believe also Jackie Barter - SCP-1903) as well as the original Crafter Clovis from SCP-2746). For whatever reason (possibly related to the manner of her original destruction) she seems able to manifest simultaneously in multiple incarnations (it's unclear to me if the other entities are also so able).

item.title: Possible link mistake?
item.content:encoded: Clovis' scp link says 1931-2 (and linked page matches)…but the description and illustration seem to suggest Clovis is supposed to be 2099-A. Am I missing something, or are the links just messed up?

item.title: ANNOUNCEMENT: Hub updated
item.content:encoded: OZ Ouroboros's Izaak from SCP-2988, has been given a character bio, and officially added to the hub!

item.title: ANNOUNCEMENT: Hub updated
item.content:encoded:
OZ Ouroboros's SCP-2988 has been officially added to the hub!

Sorry for the delay, I was indisposed for most of the week.
